checks.finding: T-001 (tasks.md:109-138) and T-002 (tasks.md:336-361) are both Risk: medium. Neither task's Scope/Done When/Goal touches the proximity-rule sentinel surfaces: both explicitly declare Data Migration: none and Breaking API: no (tasks.md:165-170, 393-398), and no auth/authz/payment/PII surface is present anywhere in requirements.md or design.md for this feature (ux-spec.md/frontend-spec.md confirm N/A - no user-facing surface). Both Risk Rationale sections use a verified three-ground argument (additive-only fail-closed change; TEST-002/003 or TEST-008/009 external negative-branch verification; TEST-004/005 or TEST-008 construction self-check) that I independently confirmed matches the same argument shape used and evidently accepted for specs/epic-159-pillar-a2/tasks.md T-003 (lines 428-439), and correctly distinguishes itself from the genuinely high-risk precedent at specs/workflow-state-integrity/tasks.md:23-26, which I confirmed by direct read is high because it CHANGED predecessor review-gate validation logic (a behavior change neither T-001 nor T-002 makes). Neither task is over-classified either: both carry substantive script/workflow/test-suite edits, not merely UI copy, CSS, or documentation-only changes.
SKIP: no Risk: high or Risk: critical task in scope. tasks.md contains only T-001 and T-002, both Risk: medium (tasks.md:109, 336).

Recounted directly: T-001's Done When has exactly 8 top-level '- [ ]' bullets (tasks.md:263-309) and T-002 has exactly 7 (tasks.md:476-509) - both at or under the '>8' threshold, resolving the round-2 11-bullet finding. T-001's Scope section literally states 'four surfaces (script edit, suite authoring, CI wiring, documentation)' (tasks.md:217-222), which textually exceeds the >3-area indicator, but this is anchored to a genuinely frozen spec-level task boundary: requirements.md Main Workflows item 1 (requirements.md:270-275, Spec-Review-Status: Passed) explicitly assigns the loop-gate edit, suite authoring, CI wiring, AND the CHANGELOG/runbook CREATE to T-001 as one unit - task-review cannot split this without re-gating an already-approved spec, which is out of this gate's scope. The round-1 oversizing finding (single-commit, 4-area bundle) is structurally mitigated by the two-commit split (Global Constraints, tasks.md:32-49): Commit A bundles script+its own TDD suite+trivial CI-array-registration (a single coherent, tightly-coupled implementation unit, consistent with this repo's universal TDD-task convention), Commit B isolates documentation as a separate concern. The compound Done-When bullet (tasks.md:287-298, labeled (a)(b)(c)(d)) is a transparent, individually-labeled enumeration, verified structurally identical to the accepted precedent at specs/epic-159-pillar-a2/tasks.md:223-232 ('Cross-cutting shares, each independently verifiable'), which I confirmed by direct read also totals exactly 8 top-level Done-When bullets using the same technique. No fragmentation indicators present (only 2 tasks, neither is a single-function/single-file-rename task).

Both functional tasks have edge-case/error-path acceptance criteria beyond the happy path. T-001/REQ-001: AC-002 and AC-003 (acceptance-tests.md:11-12) are independent red-path/fail-closed-with-zero-mutation assertions; AC-004 (no-bypass grep self-check) and AC-005 (ordering assertion) are additional construction-proof edge cases. T-002/REQ-002: AC-008 (weakened-gate escape-hatch negative scan) and AC-009 (negative-branch canary proving AC-008's check is not vacuous) directly cover the failure/tamper path. requirements.md Edge Cases section (lines 289-326) documents CI-resilience, red-suite zero-mutation, weakened-gate threat, and unchanged-trigger-surface edge cases explicitly, all mapped to the AC-NNN IDs above.

Neither T-001 nor T-002 is a dedicated test-authoring task (both are TDD-style feature tasks with embedded suites), so this check was applied to the acceptance-tests.md Test Type column entries for scope-consistency. No mismatch found: TEST-001 (integration, fixture-driven, real script) genuinely spans multiple components (script + fixture filesystem + suite invocations); TEST-004/TEST-005 (construction-proof/structural-ordering) correctly target a single file/module (scripts/bump-version.sh source) matching unit-scope; TEST-007/008/009/010 (configuration conformance / negative-branch canary) correctly target release.yml's own text structure; TEST-011/012/014 (document conformance) correctly target markdown files; every TEST-ID (TEST-001..TEST-014) references a specific AC-NNN in the acceptance-tests.md table (acceptance-tests.md:10-23). Minor non-blocking observation: several Test Type labels ('negative-branch (fixture-driven)', 'construction proof', 'hygiene / non-existence assertion') are descriptive rather than the four canonical category names; this does not create scope ambiguity since each row's Test Target column is fully concrete, so it does not rise to a Major finding.

SKIP: this check's mandatory trigger (Risk: high or Risk: critical) is absent - both T-001 and T-002 are Risk: medium. Noted for completeness, not required: both tasks nonetheless carry an explicit Rollback: field (tasks.md:172-178 for T-001, tasks.md:400-405 for T-002) describing a coordinated two-commit revert.

Shared files (tests/run-all.sh/.ps1, .github/workflows/test.yml, CHANGELOG.md, docs/contributor/release-runbook.md) are each touched by both T-001 and T-002, but every overlap is the legitimate coordinated-sharing case: each task adds only its own disjoint registration lines/sections, and T-002 declares Blockers: T-001 (tasks.md:526) which structurally serializes the create-then-append ordering, consistent with design.md's Global Constraints section (design.md:393-407) and tasks.md's own Global Constraints (tasks.md:55-73). No uncoordinated parallel overlap exists.

T-002's Blocker on T-001 (tasks.md:526) is not spurious despite T-002's own 'Depends On: none materially' text (tasks.md:371-375) explicitly stating the test content itself has no functional dependency on T-001's script edit - the Blocker exists solely to serialize the genuine shared-file coordination need (registration arrays, CHANGELOG create-then-append) verified under SCOPE-DISJOINT above, which is exactly the legitimate use case the DEPENDENCY-OVERLAP check permits. This transparency (naming the real reason for the block) reduces ambiguity rather than inflating the critical path. No missing blockers found: T-001 (Blockers: None) genuinely has no prerequisite, and no other task's scope depends on unstated prior output.

SKIP: no bugfix or debugging task in scope. Both T-001 and T-002 are net-new feature-addition tasks (adding a loop-gate prerequisite and a required CI job respectively); neither task's title, Goal, Scope, or Done When describes a bugfix, regression fix, debugging, flaky-test resolution, or incident remediation.
